title: eupatorium steviifolium var. laetevirens
intro: Eupatorium steviifolium var. laetevirens, also known as White Snakeroot, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which aid in relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and soothing muscle spasms. The health benefits of White Snakeroot include its ability to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other musculoskeletal conditions, making it a valuable natural remedy for pain management. Herbal preparations of White Snakeroot can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve for external pain relief.
